title: Cannot get to run on R-Pi4 with latest Raspberry Pi OS Lite image
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running on Raspberry Pi OS Lite. Release date: May 7th 2021. Kernel version: 5.10.
After I build podman manually (there is no package I can find even in back ports), I cannot get a container to start

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Follow [this](https://www.osradar.com/how-to-install-podman-on-debian-on-10-9/) to build podman. One note, I do not install the Golang package where it say but build it manually as a higher version is needed now
2. Install slip4nets
3. podman pull hello-world

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: /usr/bin/slirp4netns failed: "/usr/bin/slirp4netns: unrecognized option '--netns-type=path'\nUsage: /usr/bin/slirp4netns [OPTION]... PID TAPNAME\nUser-mode networking for unprivileged network namespaces.\n\n-c, --configure      bring up the interface\n-e, --exit-fd=FD     specify the FD for terminating slirp4netns\n-r, --ready-fd=FD    specify the FD to write to when the network is configured\n-m, --mtu=MTU        specify MTU (default=1500, max=65521)\n-6, --enable-ipv6    enable IPv6 (experimental)\n-h, --help           show this help and exit\n-v, --version        show version and exit\n"

**Describe the results you expected:**
I would have expected the container to run

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
All the conf files needed are not created/installed in a manual build. I copied them over from a running Ubuntu 20.14 image

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.0-dev
API Version:  3.2.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.16.5
Git Commit:   1e006a58ccf62d9def0778af550c35f5acf504ae
Built:        Sun Jun  6 03:02:11 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm
```

```
Listing... Done
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running on R-Pi 4 hardware

comments.body: How did you install slirp4netns - from the system package? Have you tried building a more recent version from source?

Yes this is definitely a to old slirp4netns version.
@dwhiteddsoft Podman will be included in debian bullseye, see https://packages.debian.org/source/testing/libpod
